What is your target MSRP for the Chevrolet Sedan (specific value or 'no preference') and how important is it to you (must-have/strongly prefer/nice-to-have/flexible)?
My target MSRP for the Chevrolet Sedan is $16,620, and it's a must-have for me. I'm fairly serious about keeping the price within this range, so it's a non-negotiable.
What is your preferred vehicle size for the Chevrolet Sedan (compact, mid-size, full-size, or 'no preference') and how important is it to you (must-have/strongly prefer/nice-to-have/flexible)?
I prefer a compact Chevrolet Sedan, and it's a must-have for me. I mentioned earlier that I want something compact and straightforward, so compact size is a non-negotiable for me.
What is your preferred model year for the Chevrolet Sedan (specific value or 'no preference') and how important is it to you (must-have/strongly prefer/nice-to-have/flexible)?
My preferred model year for the Chevrolet Sedan is 2009 or newer, and it's a must-have for me. I don't want to deal with something too old or outdated, so I'm looking for a more modern vehicle.
What is your target city mpg for the Chevrolet Sedan (specific value or 'no preference') and how important is it to you (must-have/strongly prefer/nice-to-have/flexible)?
I'm aiming for at least 28 city MPG, but I'm fairly flexible on this aspect if the year and price are right. I would say it's a nice-to-have.

base_query_sentence: I am looking for a Toyota 4dr Hatchback with a AUTOMATIC transmission and front wheel drive.

dialogue.content: I am looking for a Toyota 4dr Hatchback with a AUTOMATIC transmission and front wheel drive.
What is your target MSRP for the Toyota 4dr Hatchback (e.g. $20,000, no preference, $30,000 or less, etc.) and how important is it to you (must-have, strongly prefer, nice-to-have, or flexible)?
My target MSRP for the Toyota 4dr Hatchback is $24,495 or less. I would say this is a must-have for me, as I'm trying to keep the cost within a specific budget.

What is your preferred model year for the Toyota 4dr Hatchback (e.g. 2018, 2020, no preference, 2022 or newer, etc.) and how important is it to you (must-have, strongly prefer, nice-to-have, or flexible)?
I would prefer a model year of 2017 or newer, as I mentioned earlier. It's a must-have for me, as I want a newer car with modern efficiency and a newer feel.
What is your target city mpg for the Toyota 4dr Hatchback (e.g. 25, 30, 35, no preference) and how important is it to you (must-have, strongly prefer, nice-to-have, or flexible)?

My target city MPG for the Toyota 4dr Hatchback is at least 30, and I would say it's a must-have for me. I'm looking for a reliable and efficient daily car, and getting at least 30 city MPG is a non-negotiable for me.
What is your target highway MPG for the Toyota 4dr Hatchback (e.g. 25, 30, 35, no preference) and how important is it to you (must-have, strongly prefer, nice-to-have, or flexible)?

I'd like to have at least 36 highway MPG, and I'd say it's a nice-to-have for me. I'm willing to compromise on highway mileage, but I'm more focused on getting at least 30 city MPG, which is a must-have for me.

base_query_sentence: I am looking for a Volvo Wagon with a AUTOMATIC transmission and front wheel drive.

dialogue.content: I am looking for a Volvo Wagon with a AUTOMATIC transmission and front wheel drive.
What type of fuel does the engine use (gasoline, diesel, hybrid, or no preference), and how important is this to you (must-have, strongly prefer, nice-to-have, or flexible)?
I strongly prefer a gasoline engine that runs on regular unleaded fuel. It's a must-have for me, as I mentioned earlier that I want hassle-free ownership and easier fuel costs.
What is your target maximum budget for the Volvo Wagon (specific value or 'no preference'), and how important is it to you (must-have, strongly prefer, nice-to-have, or flexible)?
My target maximum budget for the Volvo Wagon is $32,850. I'm willing to compromise on price if the year and fuel type fit perfectly, so I'd say it's a nice-to-have.
What is your preferred vehicle size (e.g. compact, mid-size, full-size, or 'no preference'), and how important is this to you (must-have, strongly prefer, nice-to-have, or flexible)?
I'm looking for a Midsize Volvo wagon, and I'd say this is a nice-to-have for me. I'm moderately flexible on the size, as long as it still feels roomy, but Midsize is my ideal.
What is your preferred model year for the Volvo Wagon (specific value or 'no preference'), and how important is it to you (must-have, strongly prefer, nice-to-have, or flexible)?
I prefer a Volvo Wagon with a model year of 2008 or newer, as I mentioned earlier. This is a must-have for me, as I want a car that still feels reasonably modern and dependable.
